scope: Linux kernel, system services, apt, Flatpak, GNOME internals, ZenithShell, first-party apps, image build pipeline

threats.risk: man-in-the-middle update attack or malicious package metadata
mitigations: require signed repository metadata
pin trusted repository keys in the image build
block unsigned or expired metadata
show repository trust state in Zenith Packages

threats.risk: local privilege escalation through settings, package, or device-control services
mitigations: prefer dbus services with narrow polkit actions over setuid helpers
audit retained setuid binaries before release
log every privileged action through journald
keep Zenith app UIs unprivileged by default

threats.risk: sandbox escape through broad filesystem grants, portal bugs, or compositor integration
mitigations: default to least-privilege Flatpak permissions
prefer document portal access over home-directory access
surface risky permissions in Zenith Packages
keep xdg-desktop-portal-gnome updated through apt

threats.surface: GNOME Shell extension and ZenithShell customizations
threats.risk: shell crash, session compromise, or lock-screen bypass caused by custom shell code
mitigations: keep shell extension code small and reviewable
avoid lock-screen modifications in early milestones
test extension enable, disable, suspend, resume, and crash recovery
fall back to stock GNOME Shell session for recovery

threats.risk: malicious or mismatched kernel module gains kernel execution
mitigations: use distribution kernel packages and signed update channels
avoid out-of-tree drivers in the default image
record firmware and kernel package versions in image metadata
plan Secure Boot enforcement for release images

threats.risk: unbounded apt cache, Flatpak runtimes, logs, crash dumps, or snapshots consume the 30GB target
mitigations: cap apt cache and journal size
keep one mandatory rollback snapshot by default
prune unused Flatpak runtimes
fail image builds that exceed the storage budget

threats.risk: host contamination or unpinned local state changes release image contents
mitigations: build rootfs from manifests
record package list and versions
keep generated artifacts out of source control
run validation before publishing an ISO
